name: Foundry (Smart Contract Framework)

auth.notes: Local toolchain; RPC API key for mainnet forking

performance.notes: Rust-based — 10-100x faster test execution than Hardhat (JavaScript); parallel test execution

agent_readiness.notes: Rust-based Ethereum development toolkit. forge (build/test), cast (RPC interactions), anvil (local testnet), chisel (Solidity REPL). Write tests in Solidity. Fuzz testing built-in. Mainnet forking. Gas snapshots. Invariant testing. The fastest Solidity testing framework. Now the industry standard, preferred over Hardhat for most new projects. Excellent documentation.

gotchas: Solidity-only test framework — no JavaScript test logic possible
Less ecosystem integration than Hardhat for non-standard plugins
anvil (local testnet) has some edge cases with complex forks
Cheatcodes specific to Foundry — not portable to other frameworks
best_when: Smart contract development and testing (now industry standard)
Fuzz testing and invariant testing for security-critical contracts
Fast local testing without JavaScript overhead
Security auditing with built-in fuzzing capabilities
avoid_when: Teams heavily invested in Hardhat plugins ecosystem
Projects requiring JavaScript test logic for complex setup
summary: Foundry is the Rust-based Ethereum smart contract development toolkit (forge, cast, anvil, chisel). 10-100x faster than Hardhat. Write tests in Solidity, not JavaScript. Built-in fuzzing and invariant testing. Mainnet forking. Now the industry standard for new Solidity projects. Excellent documentation and active development. Best for security-focused smart contract development.
